summary: To efficiently track and manage attendance for a group of users or employees.
tagline: Effortless attendance tracking for any group.

items.name: User Registration
items.description: Allow individual employees to create a new account for the attendance tracker.

items.name: User Login
items.description: Enable registered employees to securely sign in to their personal attendance account.

items.name: Password Reset
items.description: Provide a mechanism for employees to reset their forgotten account passwords.

items.name: User Profile Management
items.description: Allow employees to view and update their personal profile information such as name and contact details.

items.name: Manual Check-in/Check-out
items.description: Enable employees to manually mark their attendance by tapping a simple button for check-in and check-out.

items.name: Location-based Check-in/Check-out
items.description: Allow employees to mark their attendance using their current GPS location for verification.

items.name: Current Attendance Status Display
items.description: Show the employee's real-time attendance status (e.g., checked in, checked out) on the main dashboard.

items.name: Daily Attendance Log View
items.description: Display a detailed chronological log of the employee's daily check-in and check-out times.

items.name: Monthly Attendance Summary View
items.description: Present a summary of the employee's total hours worked for each month.

items.name: Export Daily Attendance Log Report
items.description: Allow employees to generate and export a report of their detailed daily attendance logs.

items.name: Export Monthly Attendance Summary Report
items.description: Enable employees to generate and export a report containing their monthly attendance summaries.

items.name: Employee Dashboard
items.description: Provide a personalized home screen displaying current attendance status, quick actions, and navigation to history and reports.

items.name: Personal Attendance Rules
items.description: Allow employees to configure their personal work schedules, such as default work hours and break times.

items.name: Notification Preferences
items.description: Enable employees to manage and customize notifications for check-in/check-out reminders.

items.name: Attendance Trend Charts
items.description: Display graphical representations of attendance patterns and trends over various periods.

items.name: Biometric Authentication
items.description: Implement biometric login options (e.g., fingerprint, face ID) for quick and secure access.

screens.name: SplashScreen
screens.purpose: Displays the app logo and loads initial data/authentication status before navigating to the main app or onboarding/login.
screens.notes: Handles initial routing based on user login status.

screens.name: OnboardingScreen
screens.purpose: Introduces the app's core features and benefits to new users.
screens.notes: Shown only on first app launch.

screens.name: LoginScreen
screens.purpose: Allows registered users to securely sign in to their accounts. Integrates biometric authentication.
screens.notes: Navigates to EmployeeDashboardScreen on successful login.

screens.name: SignupScreen
screens.purpose: Enables new employees to create a new account for the attendance tracker.
screens.notes: Collects basic user information for registration.

screens.name: PasswordResetScreen
screens.purpose: Provides a mechanism for users to reset forgotten account passwords via email verification.
screens.notes: Typically involves two steps: requesting a reset link/code and then setting a new password.

screens.name: EmployeeDashboardScreen
screens.purpose: Serves as the personalized home screen for employees, displaying current attendance status, quick check-in/check-out actions, and navigation to history and reports.
screens.notes: Integrates manual and location-based check-in/out. Requires location permissions for location-based tracking.

screens.name: UserProfileScreen
screens.purpose: Allows employees to view their personal profile information.
screens.notes: Provides an option to navigate to EditUserProfileScreen.

screens.name: EditUserProfileScreen
screens.purpose: Enables employees to update their personal profile information such as name and contact details.
screens.notes: Form-based input for updating user details.

screens.name: DailyAttendanceLogScreen
screens.purpose: Displays a detailed chronological log of the employee's daily check-in and check-out times, with an option to export.

screens.name: MonthlyAttendanceSummaryScreen
screens.purpose: Presents a summary of the employee's total hours worked for each month, with an option to export.
screens.notes: Provides an overview of monthly attendance data.

screens.name: SettingsScreen
screens.purpose: A central hub for managing various app settings and preferences, including navigation to personal attendance rules and notification preferences.
screens.notes: Includes a toggle for biometric authentication.

screens.name: PersonalAttendanceRulesScreen
screens.purpose: Allows employees to configure their personal work schedules, such as default work hours and break times.
screens.notes: Helps in calculating accurate attendance summaries based on personal schedules.

screens.name: NotificationPreferencesScreen
screens.purpose: Enables employees to manage and customize notifications for check-in/check-out reminders and other alerts.
screens.notes: Allows setting specific times for reminders.

screens.name: AttendanceTrendScreen
screens.purpose: Displays graphical representations of attendance patterns and trends over various periods.
screens.notes: Uses charting libraries to visualize data (e.g., Fl_chart).

screens.name: ErrorScreen
screens.purpose: Displays a generic error message when an unexpected issue occurs, with options to retry or go back.
screens.notes: Should be a robust screen to handle various error states gracefully.

navigation.notes: The SplashScreen handles initial authentication status. If authenticated, navigate to /dashboard; otherwise, navigate to /onboarding (if first launch) or /login. An authentication guard will redirect unauthenticated users from protected routes to /login.

third_party_apis.name: Email Service
third_party_apis.purpose: Sending password reset emails and other transactional emails.

third_party_apis.name: FCM (Firebase Cloud Messaging)
third_party_apis.purpose: Sending push notifications for check-in/check-out reminders and other alerts.

third_party_apis.name: Geolocation API
third_party_apis.purpose: Backend verification of user location for location-based check-in/check-out against predefined office locations.

api_endpoints.purpose: Registers a new user account.
Authenticates a user and provides an authentication token.
Initiates the password reset process by sending a reset link/code to the user's email.
Resets the user's password using a provided token/code.
Retrieves the authenticated user's profile information.
Updates the authenticated user's profile information.
Retrieves the authenticated user's profile information.
Partially updates the authenticated user's profile information.
Retrieves the current attendance status of the authenticated user.
Records a check-in event for the authenticated user, optionally with location data.
Records a check-out event for the authenticated user, optionally with location data.
Retrieves a detailed chronological log of the authenticated user's daily attendance.
Generates and exports a report of the authenticated user's daily attendance logs.
Retrieves a summary of the authenticated user's total hours worked for a given month.
Generates and exports a report of the authenticated user's monthly attendance summaries.
Retrieves the authenticated user's personal attendance rules and work schedules.
Updates the authenticated user's personal attendance rules and work schedules.
Retrieves the authenticated user's notification preferences.
Updates the authenticated user's notification preferences.
Retrieves attendance data for the authenticated user to display trends and patterns.
Retrieves a list of all users (for admin).
Retrieves a specific user's profile information (for admin).
Updates a specific user's profile information (for admin).
Deletes a specific user account (for admin).
Retrieves daily attendance logs for all users or a specific user (for admin).
Retrieves monthly attendance summaries for all users or a specific user (for admin).
Retrieves attendance trends for all users or a specific user (for admin).
Creates a new valid check-in location (for admin).
Retrieves a list of all predefined check-in locations (for admin).
Updates an existing check-in location (for admin).
Deletes a check-in location (for admin).

database_tables.name: users
database_tables.purpose: Stores user accounts, authentication credentials, and profile information.

fields.name: id
fields.notes: Primary key for the user.

fields.name: email
fields.notes: User's email address, used for login and password reset.

fields.name: password_hash
fields.notes: Hashed password for secure authentication.

fields.name: first_name
fields.notes: User's first name.

fields.name: last_name
fields.notes: User's last name.

fields.name: role
fields.notes: User's role (e.g., 'User', 'Admin', 'Manager'). Defaults to 'User'.

fields.name: reset_password_token
fields.notes: Token used for password reset, expires after use or time limit.

fields.name: reset_password_expires
fields.notes: Timestamp when the password reset token expires.

fields.name: fcm_token
fields.notes: Firebase Cloud Messaging token for push notifications.

fields.name: created_at
fields.notes: Timestamp when the user account was created.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.notes: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

database_tables.name: locations
database_tables.purpose: Stores predefined valid physical locations where users can check-in/check-out.

fields.name: id
fields.notes: Primary key for the location.

fields.name: name
fields.notes: Name of the location (e.g., 'Main Office', 'Branch A').

fields.name: latitude
fields.notes: Latitude coordinate of the location.

fields.name: longitude
fields.notes: Longitude coordinate of the location.

fields.name: radius_meters
fields.notes: Geofence radius in meters around the coordinates for valid check-in/check-out.

fields.name: created_at
fields.notes: Timestamp when the location was created.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.notes: Timestamp when the location was last updated.

database_tables.name: attendance_records
database_tables.purpose: Stores individual check-in and check-out events for users.

fields.name: id
fields.notes: Primary key for the attendance record.

fields.name: user_id
fields.notes: Foreign key linking to the user who made the attendance record.

fields.name: check_in_time
fields.notes: Timestamp of the check-in event.

fields.name: check_out_time
fields.notes: Timestamp of the check-out event. Null if the user is currently checked in.

fields.name: check_in_latitude
fields.notes: Latitude recorded at check-in, if location tracking is enabled.

fields.name: check_in_longitude
fields.notes: Longitude recorded at check-in, if location tracking is enabled.

fields.name: check_out_latitude
fields.notes: Latitude recorded at check-out, if location tracking is enabled.

fields.name: check_out_longitude
fields.notes: Longitude recorded at check-out, if location tracking is enabled.

fields.name: check_in_location_id
fields.notes: Foreign key to the 'locations' table, indicating the predefined location for check-in.

fields.name: check_out_location_id
fields.notes: Foreign key to the 'locations' table, indicating the predefined location for check-out.

fields.name: created_at
fields.notes: Timestamp when the attendance record was created.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.notes: Timestamp when the attendance record was last updated.

flutter_architecture.state_management_reason: Riverpod provides compile-safe dependency injection and state management, which is highly scalable and testable. Its provider system is perfect for a feature-first clean architecture, allowing for easy separation of UI, logic, and data layers. The use of code generation with riverpod_generator reduces boilerplate.

App entry point for the staging environment (flavor).
App entry point for the production environment (flavor).

custom_theme_notes: Utilize Material 3 design principles for a modern look.
Implement a consistent ColorScheme and TextTheme using ThemeData.
Consider using FlexColorScheme for easier management of light and dark themes.

To find the correct local path to store the Isar database.

dev_dependencies.purpose: A build tool for running code generators (Freezed, Riverpod, Isar, etc.).
The generator part of the riverpod_annotation package.
The generator for creating immutable data classes.

The generator for creating Isar database schemas and query methods.
Recommended linting rules for Flutter apps.
A popular and simple mocking library for unit and widget tests.
A testing utility for testing Riverpod providers.

security_rules: Store authentication tokens (e.g., JWT) in flutter_secure_storage, never in SharedPreferences.
Use HTTPS for all API communication. Configure Dio to reject insecure connections.
Implement certificate pinning in production builds to prevent man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks.
Validate and sanitize all user input on both the client and server sides.
Request location permissions only when the feature is actively being used.
Obfuscate release builds using `--obfuscate` and `--split-debug-info` flags to make reverse engineering more difficult.
accessibility_notes: Ensure all interactive elements like buttons and icons have semantic labels for screen readers (e.g., `Semantics(label: 'Check In')`).
Use sufficient color contrast ratios (at least 4.5:1 for normal text) to comply with WCAG guidelines.
Wrap custom-painted widgets or complex UI elements with a `Semantics` widget to provide a meaningful description.
Ensure tap targets are at least 48x48 pixels to be easily usable.
Test the app with screen readers like TalkBack (Android) and VoiceOver (iOS).
performance_notes: Use ListView.builder or SliverList for all dynamic lists, especially attendance logs, to ensure efficient rendering.
Use `const` constructors for widgets wherever possible to reduce widget rebuilding.
Paginate API requests for fetching attendance history to avoid loading large datasets at once.
Use `.autoDispose` with Riverpod providers to automatically clean up state when it's no longer needed.
Cache user profile and other frequently accessed, non-critical data in Isar to reduce network requests and improve startup time.
Minimize the use of expensive operations like GPS lookups; only fetch location when the user initiates a check-in.
missing_info: The plan defines 'Admin' and 'Manager' user roles and includes numerous backend API endpoints for these roles, but there are no corresponding features, screens, or navigation flows defined for them in the frontend plan. The entire UI/UX is designed only for the 'User' role.
assumptions_made: It is assumed that the Admin and Manager functionalities are either out of scope for this mobile app build or will be handled through a separate web-based admin panel.
It is assumed that the duplicate user profile API endpoints ('/api/v1/users/me' and '/api/v1/user/profile') are a planning artifact and that only one set will be implemented in the final backend.
ai_notes: To improve API clarity and maintainability, consolidate the redundant user profile endpoints (e.g., `GET /api/v1/users/me` and `GET /api/v1/user/profile`) into a single, consistent set.

To fully support the 'Personal Attendance Rules' and 'Notification Preferences' features, consider adding a corresponding `user_settings` table to the database schema to formally define how this data is stored.

To make the plan fully actionable for all user types, define the screens, features, and navigation flows for the 'Admin' and 'Manager' roles. If they are not part of this app, explicitly state that they will be managed via a separate application.
validation_warnings: The primary_color (#ffffff) and background_color (#FFFFFF) are identical, which will result in invisible UI elements like buttons or app bars that use the primary color against the background.
